title: triosteum hispidum
intro: Triosteum hispidum, also known as horse's gentian or rough-fruited triosteum, is a perennial plant native to North America. Traditionally, the root of this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Triosteum hispidum include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the root is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
